_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch)
_meta.note: FL state-leg batch (Orlando/Miami-Dade): bryan-avila SD-39 R, ana-maria-rodriguez SD-40 R, doug-bankson HD-39 R (pastor), rashon-young HD-40 D (freshman 9/2025), bruce-antone HD-41 D. State tier. Evidence from flsenate.gov / myfloridahouse.gov roll calls, Digital Democracy Project, Florida Choice Tracker, Freedom Index, NRA-PVF, Equality Florida slate, Ballotpedia. Three R incumbents uniformly conservative (SB300, HB1557/SB254, HB1, HB931, HB543 YEA); two D incumbents reliably progressive (Antone NAY on SB300/H289/HB1/HB931/SB1718/HB543, PP-PAC endorsed, NRA F; Young AVR/Equality-FL sponsor). No cross-party deviations found. Restrictions (6-week ban) scored under sanctity[0] not abolition[1]; abolition/IVF/euthanasia left null where unproven.

profile.background: FL State Senator SD-39 (Hialeah/Miami-Dade) since Nov 2022; FL House HD-111 2014-2022 (Speaker pro tempore). Cuban-American; Captain, FL Army National Guard; MS Criminal Justice + MPA (FIU). Chair, Finance & Tax.

bryan-avila@FL.notes_set: FL State Senator, SD-39 (R), since Nov 2022 (House 2014-2022). [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Uniformly conservative record on documented roll calls: YEA on SB300 6-week abortion ban, HB1 universal school choice, HB931 school chaplains, HB543 permitless carry, HB3 anti-ESG; sponsored HB7 'Stop WOKE Act' and SB1264 (anti-communism curriculum); YEA on HB1557 parental-rights/classroom; anti-sanctuary (SB168), E-Verify expansion (SB664), foreign-land ban (SB264); opposed the 2018 post-Parkland red-flag package (SB7026 NO). Freedom Index 81%. No cross-party deviations. Abortion-abolition, IVF, euthanasia, CBDC, nullification cells left null (no individual evidence).

0.note: YEA on SB300 (6-week 'Heartbeat Protection Act'); also YEA on SB404 parental-consent and HB1429 dismemberment-abortion ban.

4.note: YEA on HB1557 'Parental Rights in Education' restricting classroom instruction on sexual orientation/gender identity (House, 2022).

0.note: YEA on HB1 universal school-choice vouchers (2023).

1.note: YEA on HB1557, which requires parental notification of changes in student health/services.

2.note: Named sponsor of HB7 'Stop WOKE Act' (restricts CRT/race-and-sex instruction) and SB1264 (anti-communism curriculum); YEA HB1557.

4.note: YEA on HB3 'Combating ESG Activism' (2023); YEA on SB214 2A Financial Privacy Act.
YEA on SB524 (2022) election-security act, which banned private 'Zuckerbucks' election funding and created the election-crimes office.

2.note: YEA on SB1804 (death penalty for child sex-traffickers) — proportional punishment of grave offenders.

4.note: Supports capital punishment (SB1804) — affirms the magistrate's authority to bear the sword.

2.note: YEA on SB168 ending sanctuary cities; YEA on SB1808 immigration enforcement.

4.note: YEA on SB264 banning foreign (CCP/China) ownership of Florida land/farmland (2023).

1.note: NO on SB7026 (2018 post-Parkland red-flag/firearm-restriction package); YEA on SB214 2A Financial Privacy Act.

profile.background: FL State Senator SD-40 (Monroe County + part of Miami-Dade) since 2020 (FL House 2018-2020; Doral City Council 2010-2018). Cuban-American; B.S. FIU, M.S. Nova Southeastern. Chair, Environment & Natural Resources.

ana-maria-rodriguez@FL.notes_set: FL State Senator, SD-40 (R), since 2020. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Consistently conservative on documented roll calls: YEA on SB300 6-week abortion ban (with dated floor quote 'abortion takes a human life'), SB254 ban on gender-affirming care for minors, HB1557 parental-rights, HB1 universal school choice, HB931 school chaplains, SB7054 CBDC ban, SB7050 elections omnibus, SB1718 anti-sanctuary immigration, SB264 foreign-land ban, HB543 permitless carry. No cross-party deviations. Her advance-directive bills (SB566/SB312) are NOT assisted-suicide. Interest-group letter grades, anti-ESG, IVF, nullification cells left null (no individual evidence).

0.note: YEA on SB300 (6-week ban); dated 2023 floor statement that 'abortion takes a human life.'

2.note: YEA on SB254 (2023) banning gender-affirming care for minors and affirming biological sex.

4.note: YEA on HB1557 'Parental Rights in Education' restricting classroom SOGI instruction (2022).

0.note: YEA on HB1 universal school-choice vouchers (2023).

1.note: YEA on HB1557 (parental notification of student health/services).

2.note: YEA on HB1557 restricting SOGI classroom instruction.

0.note: YEA on SB7054 (2023) banning a Central Bank Digital Currency as money under FL UCC.

3.note: YEA on SB7050 (2023) elections omnibus: tightened drop-box rules, third-party voter-registration limits, signature-match and voter-roll maintenance.

2.note: YEA on SB1718 (2023) anti-sanctuary/illegal-immigration enforcement law.

4.note: YEA on SB264 (2023) restricting foreign (CCP/China) ownership of Florida land.

profile.religion: Christian — Founder/Senior Pastor, Victory Church (World Outreach Center), Apopka
profile.background: FL State Representative HD-39 (Orange/Seminole) since Nov 2022, re-elected 2024. Senior pastor (Victory Church Apopka); former Apopka City Commissioner/vice mayor. Wife Jeri also a pastor.

doug-bankson@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-39 (R), since Nov 2022 (2nd term). [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Strongly conservative pastor-legislator: YEA on SB300 6-week ban + cosponsored/YEA H289 fetal-personhood; co-introducer HB1521 'bathroom bill' + HB641 gender-identity employment; YEA HB1 universal school choice; prime sponsor HB1119 (obscene materials to minors); co-introducer HB931 school chaplains; prime sponsor HB999 gold/silver legal tender + HB6021 bullion tax exemption + YEA CBDC ban + HB3 anti-ESG; pro-police (HB601 anti-civilian-review-board YEA, SB450 capital-jury YEA, prime sponsor HB253); YEA HB543 permitless carry + HB1543 firearm-age + SB214; NRA-endorsed; FL Right to Life + FL Family Action endorsed. Freedom Index 72-75%. Note: voted NO on HCR693 Article V convention (contested on the right; not a left deviation). No cross-party left deviations.

0.note: YEA on SB300 (6-week ban); cosponsored and YEA on H289 fetal-personhood/wrongful-death; endorsed by FL Right to Life.

2.note: Co-introducer of HB1521 (2023) requiring facilities by sex at birth; sponsored HB641 gender-identity employment bill (2026) — affirms biological sex.

4.note: Co-introduced HB1521 bathroom bill — opposes LGBTQ-identity accommodation in public policy.

0.note: Among the party-line GOP majority passing HB1 universal school-choice vouchers (House 83-27, all Republicans Yea), 2023.

3.note: Prime sponsor of HB1119 defining obscene/harmful material for K-12 school-library objections (penalizing sexualized content available to minors).
Co-introducer of HB931 volunteer school-chaplains act (2024); a sitting senior pastor.

0.note: Prime sponsor of HB999 making gold & silver legal tender + HB6021 bullion sales-tax exemption (both enacted 2025); YEA on the 2023 CBDC ban.
YEA on HB601 'Stopping Anti-Police Civilian Review Boards' (2023/2024) — affirmative pro-law-enforcement vote.

2.note: Prime sponsor HB253 (escalating vehicle/police-impersonation penalties, enacted); YEA SB450 (capital-sentencing jury) — proportional punishment.

4.note: YEA on SB450 making capital sentencing easier — affirms the magistrate's authority to bear the sword.

2.note: YEA on HB1451 denying ID cards to illegal migrants (2024) — opposes state accommodation of illegal aliens.

0.note: Among the party-line GOP majority on HB543 permitless carry (House 76-32; sole R defector Lopez identified, not Bankson), 2023; NRA-endorsed.

1.note: YEA on HB1543/HB1223 restoring 18-20 firearm-purchase rights; YEA on SB214 2A Financial Privacy Act.

profile.background: FL State Representative HD-40 (Orlando/north Orange — Pine Hills, Lockhart) since a Sept 2025 special election (succeeding LaVon Bracy Davis, whose chief of staff he was). Born 1999; B.A. Bethune-Cookman; billed as the first Gen Z Democrat in the FL Legislature.

rashon-young@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-40 (D), since Sept 2025. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Freshman with a thin roll-call record (post-dates SB300/HB543/SB1718). Scored from documented sponsorships + own stated priorities: lead sponsor of HB1419 Florida Voting Rights Act (automatic + same-day voter registration); sponsor of HB1223 (HIV-modernization) listed on Equality Florida's 2026 legislative slate; Ballotpedia Candidate Connection priorities list 'Reproductive Rights and Women's Health,' 'LGBTQ+ Equality,' and 'Gun Violence Prevention.' Endorsed by Florida Rising and Run for Something; iVoterGuide 'Liberal.' No conservative cross-party deviations found; conservative-position cells documented contrary are FALSE, the rest null.

0.note: Lists 'Reproductive Rights and Women's Health' as a campaign priority (own Candidate Connection submission) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

4.note: Sponsor of HB1223 on Equality Florida's 2026 legislative slate; lists 'LGBTQ+ Equality' as a campaign priority — supports LGBTQ identity in public policy.

1.note: Lead sponsor of HB1419 (FL Voting Rights Act) expanding access via automatic and same-day voter registration — opposite of restrictive photo-ID/citizenship-verification posture.
Lists 'Gun Violence Prevention and Public Safety' as a campaign priority — favors gun control, contrary to opposing red-flag/AWB/mag limits.

profile.background: FL State Representative HD-41 (Orlando/Orange) — long-serving Democrat (in office 2002-2006, 2012-2020, 2022-present across redistricted seats). Born 1960; B.S. Electrical Engineering, Tuskegee; former chief of staff to State Sen. Buddy Dyer.

bruce-antone@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-41 (D), long-serving. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Reliable down-the-line Democratic record with documented NAY votes: SB300 6-week ban (NAY), H289 fetal-personhood (NAY), HB1 universal vouchers (NAY), HB931 school chaplains (NAY), SB1718 anti-sanctuary immigration (NAY), HB543 permitless carry (NAY). Endorsed by Planned Parenthood PAC of Florida; NRA-PVF grade F. No cross-party deviation (specifically did NOT back school choice). HB1557 predates his 2022 return (not seated) — left null, not scored as a vote. Cells with documented contrary votes are FALSE; unverified cells null.

0.note: NAY on SB300 (6-week ban) and NAY on H289 (fetal personhood, Jan 2026).

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood PAC of Florida for the 2024 general — abortion-industry PAC support.

0.note: NAY on HB1 universal school-choice vouchers (2023).

2.note: NAY on SB1718 (2023) anti-sanctuary/illegal-immigration enforcement law.

1.note: NRA-PVF grade F — opposes pro-2A deregulation; consistent with NAY on permitless carry.
